commission.notes: 5% on all sales, no tiered structure detected. Consistent across all audit cycles.

affiliate_program.shipping: Same business day on orders before 12:00 noon ET

creative_assets.notes: Program page states team is available to assist on additional marketing content needs. Standard AvantLink creative tools (banners, product feeds, deep links) available to approved affiliates via AvantLink dashboard. No self-serve public creative library. Yazing also lists Priority Bicycles but routes all commission through their own platform.

affiliate_program.approval_requirements: Active website or social media channel with original, high-quality content focused on outdoor recreation or gear; coupon/rebate/deal-aggregation sites not accepted; reviewed case-by-case via AvantLink

product_catalog.key_differentiator: Low-maintenance Gates Carbon belt drive (rust-free, grease-free) across all models
product_catalog.flagship_ebike: Priority Current Plus E-Bike (750W mid-drive motor, electronic shifting, 600% gear range, ~$3,299)

vs_benchmark.commission: at_median — 5% matches category median; competitors Velowave (7-10%) and Velotric (7%) now benchmarking above
vs_benchmark.cookie: below_median — 21 days vs 30-day category median; 3rd consecutive audit with no improvement
vs_benchmark.aov: below_median — $550+ stated AOV for standard line vs $1,200 category median; e-bike line (~$3,299) is well above median and represents highest EPC opportunity

afare_signals.commission_note: At category median (5%). No improvement trend detected. E-bike-focused competitors (Velowave 7-10%, Velotric 7%) pulling ahead at the high end.
afare_signals.cookie_note: 21-day cookie — third consecutive Priority audit with no change. Below 30-day category median. Bicycle purchases are high-consideration (multi-session research typical). Cookie gap creates real attribution risk for affiliates.
afare_signals.aov_note: Standard bike AOV ~$550 → ~$27.50 EPC at 5%. E-bike Current Plus ~$3,299 → ~$165 EPC. E-bike content is ~6× more valuable per conversion; affiliates should be steered to e-bike content.
strengths: Unique belt-drive low-maintenance positioning — strong, repeatable content hook for lifestyle and commuter creators
Same-day shipping + 7-day support = conversion-positive story to reinforce in affiliate content
30-day return policy reduces buyer hesitation; easy to recommend confidently
Affirm financing expands addressable buyer base on higher-ticket e-bikes
AvantLink is a trusted, fraud-resistant network with robust deep-link and product-feed tooling
Costco Next listing signals mainstream brand credibility
Yazing as secondary platform offers additional affiliate discovery surface
weaknesses: 21-day cookie stagnant — below 30-day category standard for 3+ audit cycles with no improvement
5% flat commission — no tiered, performance, or seasonal bonus structure ever detected
No self-serve creative library; custom asset requests require direct outreach to Priority team
Single primary network (AvantLink) — limits affiliate discovery; Yazing secondary but routes differently
E-bike high-AOV opportunity under-promoted in affiliate materials relative to its EPC potential
opportunities: PEAK SEASON: April 17 — deep into spring buying window; affiliate content published now captures April/May demand peak
E-bike EPC play: Current Plus at $3,299 yields ~$165 EPC — among the highest available in DTC bike affiliate space
Travel/lifestyle content angle via hotel partnerships (Starwood, Nantucket) for travel and leisure creators
Urban commuter and car-free lifestyle niches growing; belt-drive = minimal maintenance = strong narrative
Kids bike segment — rust/grease-free belt drive is a unique safety and maintenance pitch for parent creator channels
Comparison content: Priority vs Rad Power / Aventon on value positioning performs well in organic search
threats: Velowave (7-10% / 60-day cookie) and Velotric (7% / 30-day) are now materially better affiliate deals in the e-bike space
Aventon (4% / 30-day) and Rad Power Bikes (5% / 30-day) match or beat Priority on cookie despite similar rates
Costco Next listing creates price-comparison risk — buyers may find the listing and bypass affiliate links
Program stagnation (3rd cycle, no changes) — risk that competitive programs siphon affiliate creator attention
No evidence of any spring 2026 promotional campaign or rate bump to capitalize on seasonal demand

changes_since_last_audit.change_summary: No changes detected. Commission (5%), cookie (21 days), network (AvantLink primary), AOV ($550+), and all program terms are unchanged from 2026-04-15 audit. Program has remained fully static with no modifications observed since at least mid-2024.
changes_since_last_audit.program_stability: high — no rate changes, network additions, creative library launches, or structural modifications across all 2026 audit cycles to date

benchmark_update.notes: bicycles_dtc category benchmarks remain accurate. uppromote.com February 2026 data confirms Priority Bicycles at 5%/21 days, consistent with current benchmarks. Velowave and Velotric data already reflected in benchmark high-end examples. No update to category-benchmarks.json required.

E-bike focus: Current Plus ~$3,299 at 5% = ~$165 EPC — coach affiliate creators to lead with e-bike content, not standard bikes
Escalate cookie concern to Priority affiliate contact: 3 consecutive audits at 21 days while category median is 30 days — a formal request for extension is now warranted
Consider flagging to LiveChannel that Velowave (7-10% / 60-day) and Velotric (7% / 30-day) now offer structurally better affiliate economics in the e-bike segment
Monitor for any spring 2026 promotional rate or creative asset drop — none detected yet; check AvantLink newsletter and program dashboard
